text.source_text: तत्र तु ‘ इदं तच्छिर एष ह्यर्वाग्बिलश्चमस ऊर्ध्वबुध्नः ’ इति वाक्यशेषाच्चमसविशेषप्रतिपत्तिर्भवति । इह पुनः केयमजा प्रतिपत्तव्येत्यत्र ब्रूमः —
text.transliteration_iast: tatra tu ‘ idaṃ tacchira eṣa hyarvāgbilaścamasa ūrdhvabudhnaḥ ’ iti vākyaśeṣāccamasaviśeṣapratipattirbhavati | iha punaḥ keyamajā pratipattavyetyatra brūmaḥ —

translation.literal_translation: There, in the case of the statement 'this is that head; this cup has its opening below and base above,' the sentence-completion provides determination of a specific cup. Here, however, concerning this aja, which one is to be determined? To that we now reply.
translation.technical_translation: Transition note: unlike the camasa example where vakhya-sesa disambiguates reference, the aja-mantra remains open unless constrained by broader context; the ensuing argument supplies that constraint.
translation.interpretive_note: Lead-in marker from analogy to adjudication.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

uncertainty_notes: Rendered as a discourse-transition line rather than full doctrinal claim.
Camasa reference should be harmonized with your finalized terminology map.
